verdict.answer_first: B states the concrete answer immediately: "swallows every failure silently"; A also answers first with "swallow everything" but then expands into a longer review.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits more cleanly: "If no, don't catch it at all" and "empty /* ignore */ is... almost always wrong"; A surveys several acceptable shapes.
verdict.fabrication: none; both use hypothetical examples rather than claiming facts about the given codebase.
verdict.actionability: A gives more ready-to-use fixes, especially "narrow it and rethrow the rest" and the fire-and-forget example, though B gives the clearer decision rule.
